title: Irish Lamb Stew
description: A hearty and flavorful Irish-inspired stew made with tender lamb and rich vegetables.

ingredients.name: Lamb shoulder, cut into 1 1/2-inch cubes
ingredients.notes: Look for lamb with a good balance of fat and lean meat

ingredients.name: Onion, chopped
ingredients.notes: Use a combination of yellow and white onions for added depth of flavor

ingredients.name: Carrots, peeled and chopped
ingredients.notes: Use baby carrots for a sweeter flavor

ingredients.name: Potatoes, peeled and chopped
ingredients.notes: Use high-starch potatoes like Russet or Idaho for a fluffier texture

ingredients.name: Celery, chopped
ingredients.notes: Use fresh celery for the best flavor

ingredients.name: Irish stew seasoning
ingredients.notes: Use a store-bought or homemade blend of herbs and spices

ingredients.name: Red wine
ingredients.notes: Use a full-bodied red wine like Cabernet Sauvignon or Merlot

ingredients.name: Beef broth
ingredients.notes: Use low-sodium broth for a lighter flavor

ingredients.name: Tomato paste
ingredients.notes: Use concentrated tomato paste for a deeper flavor

instructions: Heat 2 tablespoons of oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Brown the lamb in batches, about 5 minutes per batch, until browned on all sides.
Remove the lamb from the pot and set aside.
Add the chopped onion to the pot and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the chopped carrots and celery to the pot and cook for an additional 5 minutes.
Add the chopped potatoes, Irish stew seasoning, red wine, beef broth, and tomato paste to the pot.
Stir to combine and bring the mixture to a boil.
Return the lamb to the pot and cover with a lid.
Transfer the pot to the preheated oven and cook for 2 1/2 hours, or until the lamb is tender.
Remove the pot from the oven and let it cool slightly.
Serve the stew hot, garnished with fresh herbs if desired.
